summary: npm package abused as mutable CDN for browser page-hijack iframe redirector
details: px8my ships px.js, a browser-only script whose sole behavior is to append a full-viewport iframe to document.body pointing at https://mfyz.dygz123.cn/H.html?c=wsog, covering and hijacking any host page that loads the script (e.g. via jsDelivr at https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/px8my/px.js). The tarball also ships update_px8my.sh, an operator script that rewrites the redirect domain in px.js, bumps the patch version, runs npm publish, and purges jsDelivr's edge caches — confirming the package is maintained as a mutable malicious CDN asset, with the npm registry used as the distribution channel for a rotating page-takeover redirector targeting downstream web consumers. There are no install hooks and the script cannot run under Node; the harm surface is browser pages that pull this package via a public CDN.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
